issues.title: MVP scope unrealistic for 8 engineers in 3 months
issues.description: Q1 roadmap includes Agent Harness MVP, Eval Framework Foundation, Basic Observability, AND LLM Gateway Alpha. Each is a substantial system. With 8 engineers and realistic onboarding, context-switching, and integration overhead, delivering all four to production quality in one quarter is highly aggressive.
issues.suggested_fix: Reduce Q1 scope to Agent Harness MVP + one supporting system. Defer Gateway and full Eval to Q2.
questions: What is the actual onboarding time for new hires?
Are all 8 engineers already productive, or is this a target headcount?

issues.title: Self-verification latency impact underestimated
issues.description: The assumption states '< 20% latency overhead' for self-verification, but LLM-based verification (mentioned in Q2) requires an additional LLM call. On a 1.5s base latency, even a fast verification call adds 300-500ms (20-33%). For complex verification, this could double overall latency.
issues.suggested_fix: Define tiered verification: fast rules-based for most cases, LLM-based only for high-stakes or ambiguous outputs. Include latency budget in architecture.
questions: What percentage of outputs require LLM-based verification?
Is there a latency SLA that verification must respect?

issues.title: Self-verification doubles LLM costs in worst case
issues.description: If every agent output requires LLM-based verification, costs effectively double. The cost optimization layer (routing, caching) may be offset by verification overhead.
issues.suggested_fix: Quantify verification cost impact. Consider confidence-based routing: verify only low-confidence outputs. Include verification costs in the 40% reduction target.
questions: What is the expected verification rate (% of outputs verified)?
Does the 40% cost reduction target account for verification overhead?

issues.title: Reliability positioning may not be defensible
issues.description: Reliability is a feature, not a moat. Langfuse, Maxim, Arize are all investing heavily in reliability tooling. Anthropic itself is publishing reliability patterns. Within 12 months, 'reliability' may be table stakes, not a differentiator.
issues.suggested_fix: Identify a more defensible wedge: specific vertical (legal agents, medical agents), proprietary evaluation datasets, or unique self-correction algorithms. Reliability alone is necessary but not sufficient.
questions: What prevents well-funded competitors from matching reliability features?
Is there a vertical or domain where we can build defensible expertise?

issues.title: Team composition unclear
issues.description: The roadmap assumes specific effort allocations (e.g., '3 engineers, 6 weeks') but doesn't specify required skill sets. Building an LLM Gateway requires different skills than building an Eval Framework. With only 8 engineers total, skill gaps could be critical.
issues.suggested_fix: Define required skill matrix: infra/platform, ML/LLM, frontend, DevOps. Identify gaps against current team. Prioritize hiring to fill critical gaps.
questions: What is the current team composition by skill area?
Are any roadmap items blocked on hiring specific roles?

issues.title: Sandbox security model not specified
issues.description: Architecture mentions 'Sandbox Container' but doesn't detail the isolation model. Agent code execution is a high-risk surface. Escape vulnerabilities, resource exhaustion, and data exfiltration are all potential attack vectors.
issues.suggested_fix: Specify sandbox technology (gVisor, Firecracker, Docker with security profiles). Define resource limits, network isolation, and data access controls. Schedule security review before MVP launch.
questions: What sandbox technology will be used?
Is there a threat model for agent execution?
Will agents have network access? To what endpoints?

issues.title: Semantic caching hit rate assumption may not transfer
issues.description: Evidence cites 60-85% hit rates 'in workloads with strong semantic repetition (such as customer support FAQs)'. Agent workloads may have lower repetition, especially for complex reasoning tasks.
issues.suggested_fix: Run pilot study on representative agent workloads before committing to caching infrastructure. Set realistic target (e.g., 40% hit rate) until validated.
questions: What is the expected query diversity in target use cases?
Do we have sample data to benchmark potential hit rates?

issues.title: Eval coverage metric lacks definition
issues.description: '100% of agent behaviors have eval coverage' is stated as a success criterion, but 'behavior' is undefined. Without a taxonomy of behaviors, this metric is unmeasurable.
issues.suggested_fix: Define behavior taxonomy: tool selection, output generation, error handling, termination, etc. Create coverage matrix with specific test types per behavior category.
questions: What constitutes a 'behavior' for eval coverage purposes?
How will coverage be measured and reported?

issues.title: Multi-provider dependency creates operational complexity
issues.description: Architecture shows Claude, GPT, and Local Models. Each has different rate limits, error modes, and pricing. Failover logic and model-specific tuning add significant operational burden.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider Claude-primary strategy with fallback only for specific use cases. Reduces integration surface and operational complexity.

issues.title: Agent Marketplace in Q4 is premature
issues.description: Marketplace features (reusable templates, community contributions) require a mature platform and user base. Building marketplace infrastructure before validating core platform may waste resources.
issues.suggested_fix: Defer marketplace to Year 2. Focus Q4 on platform hardening and enterprise adoption.

risks.risk: MVP delay due to underestimated scope
risks.mitigation: Ruthless scope reduction; define non-negotiable MVP vs. nice-to-have

risks.risk: Reliability differentiation erodes as market matures
risks.mitigation: Build proprietary eval datasets, domain-specific agents, or unique self-correction IP

risks.risk: Security incident from agent sandbox escape
risks.mitigation: Invest in sandbox security early; external security audit before GA

risks.risk: Key person dependency on LLM/ML expertise
risks.mitigation: Document all ML-specific decisions; cross-train team members

risks.risk: LLM provider pricing changes disrupt cost model
risks.mitigation: Multi-provider support; local model capability as hedge

alternative_sequencing.proposal: Sequence: Harness first, then Eval, then Gateway (not parallel)
alternative_sequencing.rationale: Agent Harness is the core value. Eval validates it works. Gateway optimizes cost/latency. This is the logical dependency order. Building in parallel risks integration issues and diffuse focus.
Rules-based verification in MVP is sufficient for initial reliability claims. LLM-based verification adds cost/latency complexity that should be optimized only after baseline is stable.

open_questions: What is the actual MVP definition that would satisfy initial customers?
Which customers have committed to pilot the platform?
What is the fallback plan if Q1 MVP slips by 4+ weeks?
How will we measure 'reliability' in a way customers can verify?
What is our position on EU AI Act compliance for agent systems?
